source_pr.title: ddl,executor: enhance test for auto_increment with AUTO_ID_CACHE=1
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #xxx

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Add test for https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/38442

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [X] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: TestAlterTableAutoIDCache hardcoded `show table ... next_row_id` values (e.g. expecting a fixed AUTO_INCREMENT base), which can vary and cause CI failures; the Bazel target was also quarantined via `flaky = True` and `race = "on"`.
failure_signature: Hardcoded AUTO_INCREMENT/next_row_id expectation in TestAlterTableAutoIDCache; observed value differs in CI.
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact `show table ... next_row_id` / AUTO_INCREMENT value that is not stable across runs (it can depend on allocator/service state). This PR stabilizes the test by reading the current `next_row_id` at runtime and asserting relative increments instead of hardcoding a fixed base (and also marks the Bazel `autoidtest_test` target as flaky/race while the issue is being investigated).
fix_pattern: Avoid hardcoded AUTO_INCREMENT/next_row_id values in tests by querying the current value and asserting relative increments; quarantine Bazel target with flaky/race attributes.

evidence: evidence: flaky = True and race = "on" | tk.MustQuery("select (a & 3) from t order by 1;").Check(testkit.Rows("1", "2")) (ddl/db_integration_test.go)

files: autoid_service/autoid.go, ddl/db_integration_test.go, executor/autoidtest/BUILD.bazel, executor/autoidtest/autoid_test.go, executor/autoidtest/main_test.go, executor/ddl_test.go, executor/executor_test.go, executor/insert_test.go
